zh.Stafford, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Hammond de Pulford於1150年出生在英國爾普爾福德時，他的父親Lord Robert FitzHugh25歲，母親Elizabeth Corbet25歲。他和Margery Frodsham至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1211年之後在他的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: Hammond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Old French personal name Ha(i)mon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. It frequently developed excrescent -d, giving Hamond, Haimund, and Hawmond. Alternatively, the name could derive from the Middle English personal name Hamund (Old Norse Hámundr, composed of the elements hár ‘high’ + mund ‘protection’), which may have been used in Normandy and in 12th-century eastern England, but the former explanation is more likely. The surname was sometimes confused with Almond and Ammon .

English: in the Bradford area of Yorkshire, the name is a shortened form of Ormondroyd, formerly Hamondesrode, from a lost place in Birstall (Yorkshire), named with the Middle English (Old French) personal name Hamon (1 above) + Middle English roid, a southern Yorkshire pronunciation of Old English rod ‘clearing’.
Irish: generally an importation from England, but occasionally an adopted name for Mac Ámoinn, see McCammon .

variants.name: Hammonds
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Hammond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Almond
meanings: English:
from the Middle English personal name Almund, in which Old English Æthelmund ‘noble protector’ and Ealhmund ‘temple protector’ have coalesced.
variant of   Allman   and   Allmand   both meaning ‘German’, assimilated by folk etymology to the vocabulary word denoting the tree.

variants.name: Hammett
meanings: English (southwestern England, especialy Devon): from the Middle English personal name Hamet, a pet form of ancient Germanic Hamo (see Hammond 1).

variants.name: Hamon
meanings: Breton, French, and English (Channel Islands): from the Breton, Old French, Middle English personal name Hamon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. See also Hammond .

variants.name: Hamlin
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, from the Norman French and Middle English personal name Hamelin, a double diminutive of Hamo (see Hammond ).
French: variant of   Hamelin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Americanized form of Finnish Hämäläinen (see Hamalainen ).

variants.name: Hampson
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): patronymic from the Norman personal name Hamo, Hamon (see Hammond ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAmhsaigh ‘descendant of Amhsach’ a byname meaning ‘mercenary soldier’ or ‘messenger’, from the adjective amhasach ‘aggressive’.

variants.name: Haimes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yacov, Yosef.

English: variant, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, of Haime, of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Haim (ancient Germanic Haimo, from haim- ‘home’), often Latinized as Hamo. Compare Hammond .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Hammon
meanings: English (Middlesex and Ken): variant of   Hammond  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Hammond de Pulford was born in 1150, in Pulford, Cheshire, England, his father, Lord Robert FitzHugh, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth Corbet, was 25. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Margery Frodsham. He died after 1211, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Hammond de Pulford est né en 1150, à Pulford, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Robert FitzHugh, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Corbet, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Margery Frodsham. Il est décédé après 1211, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Hammond de Pulford 1150 in Pulford, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Robert FitzHugh 25 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Corbet 25. Er hatte mit Margery Frodsham mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb nach 1211 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Hammond de Pulford è nato il nel 1150 nel Pulford, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Lord Robert FitzHugh, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Corbet, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Margery Frodsham. Morì dopo il 1211, nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Hammond de Pulford(이)가 1150년 영국 체실르 풀퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Robert FitzHugh(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Corbet(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Margery Frodsham(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1211년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Hammond de Pulford nasceu em 1150, em Pulford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord Robert FitzHugh, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Corbet, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Margery Frodsham. Ele faleceu após 1211, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Hammond de Pulford родился в 1150 г. в местности пулфорд, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord Robert FitzHugh было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Corbet, 25. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Margery Frodsham. Он умер после 1211 г. в его родном городе.
